mapping.a_element: pulse sequences designed to cancel successive orders of a time expansion for fidelity decay (dephasing suppression)
mapping.b_element: geometric phase manipulation (Berry's phase) to control qubit coherence
geometry-dependent contributions to dephasing in the qubit

mapping.a_element: qubit fidelity as a measure of coherence

brief_justification: Paper A focuses on a universal pulse sequence method to suppress dephasing by canceling higher-order terms in a time expansion, while Paper B demonstrates geometric phase accumulation (Berry's phase) to manipulate qubit coherence. The underlying mechanisms—dynamical decoupling via pulse sequences versus geometric phase control—are fundamentally distinct, despite both addressing qubit dephasing.
